{{Quote|Several crates of spice has my employer—Nigel VI they are on, Lohopa II they need to go.|Und'l|<ref name="TSKp" />}}
'''Nigel VI''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Nigel system/Legends|Nigel system]] of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]], and was the site of a little-known [[Human/Legends|Human]] colony. The planet was somewhat cold, with glaciers, plains, and large polar [[Water/Legends|ice]] caps among the dominant topographical features. Nigel VI was governed by a [[Nigel VI republic|local republic]] that chiefly focused on combating crime. The planet did not establish trade connections with the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] until around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

==Description==
Nigel VI was located in the [[Nigel system/Legends|Nigel system]] of the [[Kira sector/Legends|Kira sector]], part of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="TEAOC" /> It was one of many [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] along the [[Enarc Run/Legends|Enarc Run]], a [[Hyperspace route/Legends|trade route]] that ran from the [[Enarc system/Legends|Enarc system]] to the [[Vondarc system/Legends|Vondarc system]]. Nigel VI was a cold world with large polar [[Water/Legends|ice]] caps, covered in glaciers and plains, and its [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] was breathable for most [[Species/Legends|species]]. A local year was 325 days long, with each day consisting of 20 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|standard]] [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]]. The [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of Nigel VI was [[Nargo (city)|Nargo]].<ref name="TSKp" />

The planet was governed by a [[Nigel VI republic|local republic]] with a large focus on combating crime. As a consequence, the planetary laws were somewhat strict and visiting [[Starship/Legends|starships]] were thoroughly inspected. Laws concerning the unauthorized export or possession of [[lexonite]]—a native [[Poison/Legends|poisonous]] substance popular among [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] groups—were especially harsh, with any infractions punished by immediate [[Death/Legends|execution]]. Most of Nigel VI's imported goods fell into the category of high technology, while the chief export was the planet's own natural resources.<ref name="TSKp" />

==History==
At some point, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] colony was established on Nigel VI, but the planet remained fairly unknown to the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Over the years, crime grew to be a large irritant to the settlers. A local republic was established to combat the issue; an endeavor at which it proved quite successful. Nigel VI did not partake in interplanetary trade until around the time of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="TSKp" />

Following the fall of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], lexonite became a favored poison among terrorists sympathetic to the Imperial cause. One such group arranged for the illegal export of a shipment of the substance through [[Und'l]], a [[Buzchub]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]]. Und'l, in turn, hired a group of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] to handle the actual transport of the lexonite.<ref name="TSKp" />

==Inhabitants==
The majority of Nigel VI's population were members<ref name="TSKp" /> of the galaxy-spanning and numerically peerless<ref name="NEGASp">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref> Human species.<ref name="TSKp" />

==Locations==
===Nargo===
{{Main|Nargo (city)|l1=Nargo}}
Nargo was the capital of Nigel VI. In accordance with the planetary government's focus on fighting crime, security was a priority for the city.<ref name="TSKp" />

==Behind the scenes==
Nigel VI was created for [[Twin Stars of Kira (sourcebook)|''Twin Stars of Kira'']] ([[1993]]), a [[roleplaying game]] supplement published by [[West End Games]]. Aside from a brief list entry for the Nigel [[Star system/Legends|system]] in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ([[2009]]), the planet has not been mentioned in any other ''[[Star Wars]]'' products.

==Appearances==
